# Project blueprint

<!-- mol:map:managed begin -->
<!-- This block is regenerated by /mol:map. Custom annotations go
     OUTSIDE these markers and are preserved across re-runs. -->

_Generated 2026-05-10 by /mol:map._

## Inventory

The repository at `/Users/roykid/work/molcrafts/molnex/` is laid out as four
sibling packages under `src/` (`molix`, `molpot`, `molrep`, `molzoo`), each
with sub-packages exposing a curated public surface via `__init__.py`
re-exports. Mirror tests live under `tests/test_<package>/test_<subpkg>/`.

## molix — Training infrastructure

### molix (top-level)
- Modules: __init__.py, config.py, compile.py, logger.py, logging.py
- Public surface: Stage, TrainState, Checkpoint, StepResult, Trainer,
  CheckpointBackend, TorchSaveBackend, MSELoss, MAELoss, WeightedLoss,
  config, logger, logging, maybe_compile, ProfilerHook, JournalHook
- Style: stdlib-compatible logging facade with channels (metrics/events/
  info/warn); eagerly loads C++ ops at import; raises build instructions
  on miss
- Layer: foundation tier of the Module Dependency Graph;
  molix.config = global dtype singleton

### molix.core
- Modules: state.py, trainer.py, hook.py, metrics.py, protocols.py,
  checkpoint/, losses/, steps/
- Public surface: TrainState/Stage/StepResult/resolve/display + namespace
  machinery; Trainer; Hook Protocol + BaseHook + ScalarHook (contract
  layer only — concrete hooks live in molix.hooks); Metric/MAE/RMSE/MSE/
  R2Score/Accuracy/MetricCollection; Checkpoint + CheckpointBackend +
  TorchSaveBackend + capture_rng_states/restore_rng_states; MSELoss/
  MAELoss/WeightedLoss + energy_mse/energy_force_mse molecular presets;
  Step/DefaultTrainStep/DefaultEvalStep/extract_model_inputs/
  batch_to_device
- Style: pure contract / orchestration layer — `core/hook.py` holds ONLY
  the Protocol + BaseHook + ScalarHook (no concrete hooks remain here);
  hook namespace contract enforced via TrainState.__setitem__ rejecting
  slash/tuple writes; homegrown metrics shape-compatible with
  torchmetrics but not importing it
- Layer: top execution layer of CLAUDE.md's Module Dependency Graph; the
  contract-side dependency-target for molix.hooks and molix.io

### molix.hooks
- Modules: __init__.py, _utils.py, checkpoint.py, gpu.py, journal.py,
  profiler.py, progress.py, scalar.py, tensorboard.py, training.py
- Public surface: ActivationCheckpointingHook, CheckpointHook,
  GPUMemoryHook, GPUUtilsHook, GradClipHook, JournalHook, Log,
  MetricsHook, ProfilerHook, ProgressBarHook, StepSpeedHook,
  TensorBoardHook
- Style: every concrete hook subclasses `molix.core.hook.BaseHook`;
  legacy `*Hook` suffix retained intentionally during the hook-naming
  migration window; private `_utils._as_scalar` is the only shared
  helper; dependency direction `hooks/ → io/ + core/` only — never the
  reverse
- Layer: concrete-hook layer split out of the old monolithic
  `core/hooks.py` into a package; consumed by Trainer through the
  `Hook` Protocol

### molix.io
- Modules: __init__.py, reader.py, writer.py
- Public surface: JournalReader, JournalWriter
- Style: replaces the old `molix.recorder` planned tree — concrete
  first-party Zarr v3 sharded writer + reader, no schema dataclass and
  no Protocol indirection (the recorder spec's MetricRecord/MetricStore
  Protocols were YAGNI'd away when only one producer materialised);
  five reserved `type` strings (`scalar`, `histogram`, `text`,
  `image_ref`, `json`) dispatched via `match` inside
  `JournalWriter.append`; chunks=(1024,), shards=(1_048_576,) for HPC
  inode discipline matching PackedCache
- Layer: persistence side-tier; written by `molix.hooks.journal.JournalHook`,
  consumed by external dashboards/notebooks via `JournalReader`

### molix.data
- Modules: types.py, source.py, task.py, tasks/, pipeline.py, cache.py,
  dataset.py, datamodule.py, collate.py
- Public surface: AtomData/EdgeData/GraphData/GraphBatch; DataSource +
  InMemorySource + SubsetSource; Task/SampleTask/DatasetTask/BatchTask/
  Runnable; AtomicDress/ConstantLabel/NeighborList/UnitConvert; Pipeline/
  PipelineSpec/TaskEntry; PackedCache; BaseDataset/MmapDataset/
  CachedDataset/SubsetDataset; DataModule/DataModuleProtocol;
  collate_molecules/TargetSchema/DEFAULT_TARGET_SCHEMA
- Style: two-tier data contract (flat-dict pre-collate, nested TensorDict
  post-collate); OOP encapsulation per feedback_oop_style — no free-
  function `cache`/`execute`/`ddp` exposure, all orchestration on
  `PipelineSpec` + `PackedCache`; PackedCache deliberately rejects
  `TensorDict.memmap_()` (HPC inode rationale documented in cache.py)
- Layer: data layer — feeds into Trainer; collate boundary is the
  flat→nested handoff

### molix.datasets
- Modules: qm9.py, revmd17.py, threebpa.py
- Public surface: QM9Source, RevMD17Source, ThreeBPASource (each with
  TARGET_SCHEMA + download() classmethod)
- Style: each source is a class with class-attr TARGET_SCHEMA + download
  classmethod; private parser helpers
- Layer: concrete data source layer; consumed by Pipeline to materialise
  PackedCache files

### molix.nn
- Modules: locality.py, mlp.py, scatter.py
- Public surface: NeighborList, KeyedMLP/KeyedMLPSpec, ScatterSum,
  BatchAggregation
- Style: nn.Module wrappers around `molix.F` functional primitives;
  Pydantic Spec dataclasses
- Layer: stateful nn.Module layer between molix.F and molrep/molzoo

### molix.F
- Modules: locality.py, pot.py, scatter.py
- Public surface: get_neighbor_pairs, pme_direct, pme_reciprocal,
  scatter_sum, batch_add
- Style: stateless functional API over `torch.ops.molix` C++ kernels +
  torch-native scatter primitives
- Layer: lowest-level functional layer — wraps `molix.op` C++ ops

### molix.op
- Modules: CMakeLists.txt, include/molix/accessor.cuh,
  src/molix_library.cpp, src/molix_meta.cpp,
  src/neighbors/get_neighbor_pairs.{cpp,cu},
  src/pme/pme_{cpu.cpp,cuda.cu}
- Public surface: `torch.ops.molix::get_neighbor_pairs / pme_direct /
  pme_reciprocal` (registered into PyTorch dispatcher)
- Style: single CMake target, CPU/CUDA backends; ops registered via
  `TORCH_LIBRARY`; meta-tensor implementations registered separately
- Layer: native compute kernel layer — only place C++/CUDA appears

### molix.profiler
- Modules: _utils.py, task.py, module.py, dataloader.py, mock.py
- Public surface: TaskProfiler/ModuleProfiler/DataLoaderProfiler +
  TaskResult/ModuleResult/DataLoaderResult; MockBatch/MockSource
- Style: OOP profiler suite; `.run()` returns Result dataclass with
  `print_report()`; CUDA timer via `torch.cuda.Event`
- Layer: diagnostic side-tier — never imported by training code

## molpot — Potential functions

### molpot (top-level)
- Modules: __init__.py
- Public surface: BasePotential; LJ126/lorentz_berthelot;
  BondHarmonic/AngleHarmonic/DihedralHarmonic;
  RepulsionExp6/DispersionC6/ChargeTransfer/Polarization;
  geometric_arithmetic_mixing; AtomicEnergyMLP/EnergyHead/TypeHead;
  EnergyAggregation/ForceDerivation/StressDerivation;
  LayerPooling/EdgeToNodePooling/SumPooling/MeanPooling/MaxPooling;
  LJParameterHead/RepulsionParameterHead/ChargeTransferParameterHead/
  ChargeHead/TSScalingHead/MultiHead/PotentialComposer
- Style: aggregator `__init__` that promotes one or two symbols from each
  sub-package; sub-packages are the editing surface, not this file
- Layer: package-aggregator tier

### molpot.potentials
- Modules: base.py, mixing.py, nonbonded.py, polarization.py, angles/,
  bonds/, dihedrals/, electrostatics/, vdw/
- Public surface: BasePotential; LJ126/lorentz_berthelot;
  BondHarmonic/AngleHarmonic/DihedralHarmonic;
  RepulsionExp6/DispersionC6/ChargeTransfer + repulsion_mixing/
  dispersion_mixing/ct_mixing; Polarization; EwaldMultipoleEnergy/
  EwaldMultipoleEnergySpec; geometric_arithmetic_mixing
- Style: BasePotential = nn.Module + ABC; forward() returns scalar
  energy; calc_forces() via `torch.autograd.grad`; each potential carries
  its own mixing_fn for per-atom → per-pair param expansion; sub-folders
  categorise by physical interaction class
  (angles/bonds/dihedrals/electrostatics/vdw); each sub-folder __init__
  promotes its single canonical class
- Layer: concrete potential terms

### molpot.potentials.{angles,bonds,dihedrals,vdw}
- Modules: harmonic.py (or lj126.py for vdw)
- Public surface: AngleHarmonic / BondHarmonic / DihedralHarmonic /
  (LJ126 + lorentz_berthelot)
- Style: one file per potential family; class name encodes the form
- Layer: leaf potential terms

### molpot.potentials.electrostatics
- Modules: ewald.py
- Public surface: EwaldMultipoleEnergy, EwaldMultipoleEnergySpec
- Style: LES screened-Coulomb σ-Gaussian electrostatics with permanent
  multipoles `(q, μ, Q)` and inline non-self-consistent linear response
  from `(κ, α)`; periodic = half-k-sphere reciprocal sum, non-periodic
  = O(N²) erf-screened pairwise; verified against
  `github.com/ChengUCB/les`; Pydantic Spec
- Layer: long-range electrostatic potential — the LES landing alongside
  autograd-CG `polarization.py`

### molpot.heads
- Modules: edge.py, electrostatics.py, element.py, energy.py,
  multipole.py, rescale.py, type.py
- Public surface: AtomicEnergyMLP/EnergyHead; EdgeEnergyHead;
  HardnessHead/HardnessHeadSpec/PolarizabilityHead/PolarizabilityHeadSpec;
  ElementAlphaTable/ElementChargeTable;
  PermMultipoleHead/PermMultipoleHeadSpec;
  GlobalRescale/PerSpeciesScaleShift; TypeHead
- Style: plain nn.Module heads; Pydantic Spec dataclasses; hardness/
  polarizability heads feed `EwaldMultipoleEnergy`'s linear-response
  branch; ElementChargeTable/ElementAlphaTable are Z-indexed embedding-
  style baselines verified against `ChengUCB/les`
- Layer: prediction-head layer — feeds PotentialComposer; LES
  electrostatic-side heads coexist with the existing ML-prediction heads

### molpot.composition
- Modules: composer.py, heads.py, multihead.py, pooling.py
- Public surface: PotentialComposer;
  LJParameterHead/RepulsionParameterHead/ChargeTransferParameterHead/
  ChargeHead/TSScalingHead; MultiHead
- Style: PotentialComposer is a thin nn.Module orchestrator —
  head → params → potential.mixing_fn → energy → sum + optional autograd
  forces; pooling.py here is composer-internal feature pooling
  (LayerPooling lives canonically in molpot.pooling.layer)
- Layer: PotentialComposer tier — bridges encoder output and potentials

### molpot.derivation
- Modules: energy.py, force.py, stress.py
- Public surface: EnergyAggregation, ForceDerivation, StressDerivation
- Style: each is nn.Module; force/stress use `torch.autograd.grad` with
  `create_graph=self.training`
- Layer: autograd forces / virial — physical-quantity derivation between
  heads and loss

### molpot.pooling
- Modules: layer.py, edge.py, graph.py
- Public surface: LayerPooling, EdgeToNodePooling, SumPooling/MeanPooling/
  MaxPooling
- Style: plain nn.Module adapters for encoder-output → atom/edge/graph
  reductions; one-word filenames (`layer`/`edge`/`graph`) matching the
  recent rename pass
- Layer: adapter layer between molzoo encoders and molpot composer/heads

### molpot.graph
- Modules: radius.py
- Public surface: radius_graph
- Style: single utility free function — edge-index from positions +
  cutoff via `molix.F.locality.get_neighbor_pairs`
- Layer: graph-construction utility — molpot-side analogue of
  molix.nn.locality

## molrep — Representation learning

### molrep (top-level)
- Modules: __init__.py, README.md, docs/
- Public surface: JointEmbedding, SphericalHarmonics, BesselRBF,
  CosineCutoff, PolynomialCutoff, ScalarHead, ProductHead,
  masked_sum_pooling, masked_mean_pooling
- Style: thin re-export façade over the four sub-packages
- Layer: package-aggregator tier

### molrep.embedding
- Modules: node.py, angular.py, radial.py, cutoff.py, mlp.py
- Public surface: JointEmbedding, SphericalHarmonics, BesselRBF,
  CosineCutoff, PolynomialCutoff (note: ScalarMLPFunction in mlp.py is
  internal, not re-exported here)
- Style: equivariant embeddings on cuequivariance/cuequivariance_torch;
  Pydantic Spec construction; SphericalHarmonics consumes
  `bond_diff = pos[target] − pos[source]` per edge convention; mlp.py
  is the variance-preserving MLP shared with molzoo encoders + molpot
  heads
- Layer: Embedding stage of the embedding → interaction → readout pipeline

### molrep.interaction
- Modules: aggregation.py, contraction.py, element.py, linear.py,
  product.py, radial.py
- Public surface: MessageAggregation/MessageAggregationSpec,
  EquivariantLinear, ConvTP/ConvTPSpec,
  SymmetricContraction/SymmetricContractionSpec,
  ElementUpdate/ElementUpdateSpec,
  RadialWeightMLP/RadialWeightMLPSpec, irreps_from_l_max,
  sh_irreps_from_l_max
- Style: nn.Module + Pydantic Spec; tensor-product convolutions use
  cuequivariance Irreps + EquivariantPolynomialTP; aggregation uses
  scatter-sum on `edge_index[:,1]` (target) per edge convention
- Layer: Interaction stage of the pipeline

### molrep.readout
- Modules: pooling.py, product.py, projection.py
- Public surface: masked_sum_pooling, masked_mean_pooling,
  BasisProjection/BasisProjectionSpec, ProductHead/ProductHeadSpec
- Style: nn.Module + Pydantic Spec; pooling exposes free-function pair
  only (the previous ScatterPooling counterpart has been removed in the
  rename pass)
- Layer: Readout stage; physical-quantity heads (energy/force/stress)
  are in molpot, not here

### molrep.heads
- Modules: scalar.py, labeler.py, type.py
- Public surface: ScalarHead, TypeHead, Labeler, ProxyLabeler
- Style: generic post-readout heads; plain nn.Module; one-word filenames
- Layer: auxiliary heads — complementary to molpot.heads (which are
  physically-typed)

### molrep.utils
- Modules: equivariance.py, geometry.py
- Public surface: NeighborGraphBuilder, SphericalBasis, GaussianRBF,
  CosineCutoff
- Style: free-function helpers; geometry duplicates a non-equivariance
  fallback subset; equivariance.py provides rotation helpers used in
  symmetry tests
- Layer: utility tier — used by tests + non-cuEquivariance code paths

## molzoo — Pre-built encoders

### molzoo (top-level)
- Modules: __init__.py, mace.py, allegro.py, README.md
- Public surface: MACE, MACESpec, Allegro, AllegroSpec — accept
  `(Z, bond_dist, bond_diff, edge_index)` →
  `(N, num_layers, num_features)`
- Style: TensorDictModuleBase subclass per encoder; Pydantic Spec; module
  docstring carries paper DOI/arXiv per the Scientific Correctness rule;
  Allegro is a faithful port of `mir-group/allegro` (see specs/allegro.md
  §9 for pinned commit), MACE composes molrep blocks
- Layer: encoder-only tier — produces raw features; readout/potentials
  are downstream in molpot

### molzoo.specs
- Modules: allegro.md (only spec currently checked in)
- Public surface: per-encoder spec markdown, 10 immutable sections (Scope/
  Paper↔Code Mapping/Reference Alignment/Adaptation Ledger/Math Contract/
  Config Mapping/Benchmark Contract incl. §7.4 run log/System Boundary/
  Version Pinning/Drift Policy)
- Style: append-only §7.4 run log; authored by `/molzoo-spec`; audited by
  `molzoo-auditor` (📝/🆚 patch spec, ⚠️ print to chat); status
  draft→partial→ready gates code authoring
- Layer: documentation/contract layer paired one-to-one with each encoder
- KNOWN GAP: `mace.md` is absent despite `mace.py` existing — violates
  the "one spec per encoder" rule in CLAUDE.md (`molzoo/README.md`
  already marks it `*(todo)*`)

## Tests

- Mirrors `src/` tree:
  `tests/test_molix/{test_core,test_data,test_datasets,test_hooks,test_io,test_nn}`,
  `tests/test_molpot/{test_composition,test_heads,test_les_parity,test_potentials,test_readout}`,
  `tests/test_molrep/{test_embedding,test_interaction,test_readout}`,
  `tests/test_molzoo/test_{allegro,mace,mace_encoder,symmetry}.py`
- New `test_io/` (writer/reader/docstring) and
  `test_hooks/test_reexports.py` directories track the
  `recorder/ → io/` rename and `core/hooks.py → hooks/` package split;
  `test_les_parity/` covers LES forward/kfac/make_kernels/
  self_contribution against the ChengUCB/les oracle
- `tests/_oracles/screened_coulomb.py` provides the LES reference values;
  `tests/symmetry_helpers.py` and `tests/utils.py` host shared rotation
  / fixture utilities; pytest with parametrised tests + conftest
  fixtures; CI runs CPU-only pytest job (no coverage gate)

<!-- mol:map:managed end -->
